eth: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ethiopian Sign Language.
heh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hehe.
heir: noun. Someone who inherits, or is designated to inherit, the property of another.
her: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Herero.
heriot: noun. The return of military equipment.
hero: noun. Somebody who possesses great bravery and carries out extraordinary or noble deeds.
heth: noun. The eighth letter of many Semitic alphabets (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
hie: verb. To hasten; to go quickly, to hurry.
hire: noun. A person who has been hired, especially in a cohort.
hit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hittite.
hither: adverb. To this place, to here.
hitherto: adverb. Up to this or that time.
hitter: noun. One who or that which hits.
hoe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Horom.
hot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hote.
hotter: adjective. comparative form of hot: more hot
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
otter: noun. An aquatic or marine carnivorous mammal in the subfamily Lutrinae.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
riot: noun. A tumultuous disturbance of the public peace by a large group of people, often involving violence or damage to property.
roe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ronji.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rote: noun. Mechanical routine; a fixed, habitual, repetitive, or mechanical course of procedure.
roti: noun. A kind of unleavened flatbread commonly consumed in South Asia and the Caribbean.
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
teth: noun. The ninth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
the: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chitwania Tharu.
their: determiner. Belonging to, from, of, or relating to, them (plural).
thorite: noun. A radioactive mineral, composed mostly of thorium silicate (Th Si O 4), along with silicates of uranium and the rare earths.
throe: noun. A severe pang or spasm of pain, especially one experienced when the uterus contracts during childbirth, or when a person is about to
tire: verb. To become sleepy or weary.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titer: noun. The concentration of a substance as determined by titration.
titre: noun. The strength or concentration of a solution that has been determined by titration.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
tort: noun. A wrongful act, whether intentional or negligent, regarded as non-criminal and unrelated to a contract, which causes an injury and
torte: noun. A rich, dense cake, typically made with many eggs and relatively little flour (as opposed to a sponge cake or gâteau).
tot: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Patla-Chicontla Totonac.
toter: noun. One who totes or carries something.
tret: noun. An allowance to purchasers, for waste or refuse matter, of four pounds on every 104 pounds of suttle weight, or weight after the tare
trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
trite: adjective. used so many times that it is commonplace, or no longer interesting or effective; worn out, hackneyed.
trot: noun. A gait of a person or animal faster than a walk but slower than a run.
troth: noun. An oath, pledge, plight, or promise.
